content.problemStatement:
You are given two integers NNN and XXX. Output an array AAA of length NNN such that:


−1000≤Ai≤1000-1000 \le A_i \le 1000−1000≤Ai​≤1000 for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.
All AiA_iAi​ are **distinct**.
mean(A)=X\texttt{mean}(A) = Xmean(A)=X.
If there are multiple answers, print any. It is guaranteed that under the given constraints at least one array satisfying the given conditions exists.

As a reminder, the mean of an array BBB of size MMM is defined as: mean(B)=∑i=1MBiM\texttt{mean}(B) = \dfrac{\sum_{i = 1}^{M} B_i}{M}mean(B)=M∑i=1M​Bi​​.

For example, mean([3,1,4,8])=3+1+4+84=164=4\texttt{mean}([3, 1, 4, 8]) = \frac{3 + 1 + 4 + 8}{4} = \frac{16}{4} = 4mean([3,1,4,8])=43+1+4+8​=416​=4.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX - the size of the array AAA and the mean of the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output an array AAA of length NNN which satisfies the given conditions.
